refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify it remains within the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system modules.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
DP2: Use dedicated high-reliability network link with QoS prioritization.
DP3: Integrate packet loss monitoring and dynamic adjustment algorithms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol and network link quality.
Dependency on real-time monitoring data from telemetry.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates communication quality management, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmissions affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding latency constraints in future FR refinements to balance packet loss and responsiveness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing teleoperation communication protocols and real-time data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection used for teleoperation data transmission.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send packet loss and communication quality metrics to monitoring and logging systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands and task instructions.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused containment of communication protocol logic.
Vehicle module primarily handles physical control, less suitable for communication protocol containment.
Highest containment score; communication protocol logic naturally fits here with minimal diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not communication protocol.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing communication protocol management within the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overload of Teleoperation module if communication protocol complexity grows.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor Teleoperation module complexity and consider splitting communication protocol into submodules if needed.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with a clear mechanism to control packet loss.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion as communication protocol logic is contained within Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores strongly favor Teleoperation module, supporting architectural clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via Telemetry interface during teleoperation tests.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add latency and responsiveness constraints in FR refinements.
Plan for scalability of Teleoperation module to handle communication protocol complexity.

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment satisfy the requirement with clear verification paths and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system modules.

revise.why: To incorporate latency and responsiveness constraints in future refinements.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Customer-defined packet loss limits are achievable with adaptive protocols.
open_risks: Potential complexity growth in Teleoperation module.
evidence_needed: Test data on packet loss and latency under representative network conditions.
Telemetry logs confirming real-time monitoring accuracy.
